title: Today's Tech Brief: Technology platforms keep shifting around infrastructure
summary: A look at the latest tech news, focusing on platform shifts, infrastructure, and cloud economics.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the latest tech news. Today, I'm focusing on how technology platforms are shifting around infrastructure. It's a complex topic, but I'll try to break it down in simple terms. I've been following a few key developments that I think are worth exploring.

First up, Oracle is making a big bet on AI with a new Nvidia DGX Cloud service. This is a cloud-based infrastructure specifically designed for AI workloads. What stands out to me is that Oracle is trying to catch up in the AI space by leveraging Nvidia's powerful hardware. It's like they're trying to turbocharge their cloud offerings. I'm curious to see how this plays out, as AI is an area where Oracle has been investing heavily.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
